observation: I can see a very long country dropdown list is open, showing many countries from Afghanistan to Zimbabwe. The page appears to be showing a country selection dropdown for sign-up, with countries listed alphabetically. I can see many options including Indonesia, Japan, Germany, and other suitable countries that are typically not restricted on financial platforms. There's also a 'Sign up now' button at the bottom. I need to select a country that is likely to be accepted.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user, I need to select a country to proceed with sign-up. Looking at the visible countries, I should choose one that is typically accepted on financial platforms. Indonesia (element 209) appears to be a good choice as it's commonly accepted and I can see it clearly in the list. This will allow me to proceed with the registration.
ux_issues: Very long scrollable country list could be overwhelming for elderly users
Small text size in country options may be difficult to read
No clear indication of which countries might be restricted
